QF.id: XXVA Sales and Distribution of Research Services_7

question.text: Data visualization in research reports is recommended because:

answer.text: It makes the report longer and more impressive
Visual data (charts, graphs) is easier to understand than written numbers and enhances communication of analysis
SEBI mandates a minimum of 10 charts per research report
It replaces the need for written analysis and commentary

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Data visualization through charts and graphs makes complex financial data more accessible and easier to understand. It enhances the communication effectiveness of the research report. **The Trap:** There's no SEBI mandate on minimum charts. Visualization supplements written analysis — it doesn't replace it. Length isn't a quality indicator. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XV Chapter 13 recommends data visualization as a best practice for research reports. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When presenting research to clients, lead with the visual summary — charts showing price trends, financial ratios, and peer comparisons quickly convey the core thesis before diving into detailed analysis.
